claimText: The SpaDeX Mission by ISRO demonstrates the rendezvous and docking of two small spacecraft, SDX01 (Chaser) and SDX02 (Target), in a low-Earth circular orbit.
evidenceExcerpt: Objective: To develop and demonstrate the technology needed for rendezvous, docking, and undocking of two small spacecraft (SDX01 (Chaser) and SDX02 (Target)) in a low-Earth circular orbit.
examUse: Statement-based question on the objective and orbit type of the SpaDeX mission.
distractorHooks: Geostationary orbit instead of low-Earth orbit
Lunar orbit instead of Earth orbit

claimText: The indigenous docking technology developed by ISRO for joining two fast-moving satellites in space is known as the Bhartiya Docking System.
evidenceExcerpt: The docking technology (joining of two fast-moving satellites in space), indigenously developed by ISRO, is known as the Bhartiya Docking System.
distractorHooks: Naming it Gaganyaan Docking System or Vikram Docking System
Attributing the technology to a foreign space agency collaboration

claimText: With the SpaDeX Mission, India became the fourth country in the world to achieve satellite docking in space, after the US, Russia, and China.
evidenceExcerpt: The mission made India the fourth country in the world to achieve satellite docking in space, joining the ranks of the US, Russia, and China.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's global standing in space docking technology.
distractorHooks: India being the third or fifth country
Including ESA or Japan in the list of predecessors

claimText: The spacecraft for the SpaDeX Mission were designed by the UR Rao Satellite Centre (URSC).
evidenceExcerpt: The SpaDeX spacecraft were designed by the UR Rao Satellite Centre (URSC).
examUse: Institution-feature question matching space missions to their designing centers.
distractorHooks: Designed by Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre (VSSC)
Designed by Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC)
